triple_conseq_frame.solution:
Lemma triple_conseq_frame : forall H2 H1 Q1 t H Q,
  triple t H1 Q1 ->
  H ==> H1 \* H2 ->
  Q1 \*+ H2 ===> Q ->
  triple t H Q.
Proof using.
  introv M WH WQ. applys triple_conseq.
  { applys triple_frame M. }
  { applys WH. }
  { applys WQ. }
Qed.
triple_conseq_frame.explanation: Combine consequence and frame rules. First frame M with H2, then use consequence to adjust pre/postconditions.

triple_hpure'.solution:
Lemma triple_hpure' : forall t (P:Prop) Q,
  (P -> triple t \[] Q) ->
  triple t \[P] Q.
Proof using.
  introv M. applys triple_hpure. intros HP.
  applys triple_conseq. { applys M HP. }
  { xsimpl. } { xsimpl. }
Qed.
triple_hpure'.explanation: Extract the pure fact P, then use the hypothesis to get a triple with empty precondition, and adjust via consequence.

triple_hexists.solution:
Lemma triple_hexists : forall t (A:Type) (J:A->hprop) Q,
  (forall x, triple t (J x) Q) ->
  triple t (\exists x, J x) Q.
Proof using.
  introv M. intros h (x&Hx). applys M Hx.
Qed.
triple_hexists.explanation: To prove a triple with an existential precondition, destruct the existential witness and use the hypothesis.

triple_named_heap.solution:
Lemma triple_named_heap : forall t H Q,
  (forall h, H h -> triple t (= h) Q) ->
  triple t H Q.
Proof using.
  introv M. intros h Hh. applys M Hh. auto.
Qed.
triple_named_heap.explanation: Name the heap h satisfying H, then use the hypothesis with the exact heap predicate (= h).

triple_hexists2.solution:
Lemma triple_hexists2 : forall A (Hof:A->hprop) (Qof:A->val->hprop) t,
  (forall x, triple t (Hof x) (Qof x)) ->
  triple t (\exists x, Hof x) (\exists x, Qof x).
Proof using.
  introv M. applys triple_hexists. intros x.
  applys triple_conseq. { applys M. }
  { xsimpl. } { intros v. xsimpl. }
Qed.
triple_hexists2.explanation: Extract the existential from the precondition, apply the per-witness triple, then pack the existential into the postcondition.

triple_hexists_of_triple_hexists2.solution:
Lemma triple_hexists_of_triple_hexists2 : forall t (A:Type) (Hof:A->hprop) Q,
  (forall x, triple t (Hof x) Q) ->
  triple t (\exists x, Hof x) Q.
Proof using.
  introv M. applys triple_conseq (triple_hexists2 M).
  { xsimpl. }
  { intros v. xsimpl. intros x. auto. }
Qed.
triple_hexists_of_triple_hexists2.explanation: Derive triple_hexists from triple_hexists2 by collapsing the existential postcondition via consequence.

seval_val_inv.solution:
Lemma seval_val_inv : forall s v Q,
  seval s (trm_val v) Q ->
  Q v s.
Proof using.
  introv M. destruct M as (HR&HS&HC). applys HC.
  { applys steps_refl. }
  { intros t' s' R. inverts R. false. inverts H. }
Qed.
seval_val_inv.explanation: A value is already fully evaluated. Extract correctness from seval, apply it with zero steps, and show values are not reducible.

seval_terminates.solution:
Lemma seval_terminates : forall s t Q,
  seval s t Q ->
  exists v s', steps s t s' (trm_val v).
Proof using.
  introv (HR&HS&HC). induction t; try solve [exists* _ s].
  { applys HS. }
Qed.
seval_terminates.explanation: seval implies termination: extract the termination component from seval and provide the witness.

seval_safe.solution:
Lemma seval_safe : forall s t Q,
  seval s t Q ->
  safe s t.
Proof using.
  introv (HR&HS&HC). intros s' t' R.
  tests C: (trm_is_val t').
  { left. auto. }
  { right. applys HS. applys R. }
Qed.
seval_safe.explanation: seval implies safety (never stuck): for any reachable configuration, either it's a value or it can step further.

seval_correct.solution:
Lemma seval_correct : forall s t Q,
  seval s t Q ->
  correct s t Q.
Proof using.
  introv (HR&HS&HC). intros v s' R. applys HC R.
  intros t' s'' R'. inverts R'. false. inverts H.
Qed.
seval_correct.explanation: seval implies correctness: if t steps to value v in state s', then Q v s' holds.

seval_seq.solution:
Lemma seval_seq : forall s t1 t2 Q1 Q,
  seval s t1 Q1 ->
  (forall v1 s2, Q1 v1 s2 -> seval s2 t2 Q) ->
  seval s (trm_seq t1 t2) Q.
Proof using.
  introv M1 M2. destruct M1 as (HR1&HS1&HC1). splits.
  { intros s' t' R. inverts R; try solve [inverts H].
    tests C: (trm_is_val t1').
    { destruct C as (v1&->). lets (HR2&HS2&HC2): M2 (HC1 (steps_refl _ _)
        (fun t' s' R => inverts R; [false; inverts H | ])).
      inverts H0. applys HS2. applys steps_refl. }
    { applys HS1. auto. } }
  { lets (v&s'&R): HR1. exists v. exists s'. applys steps_of_step.
    applys step_seq. }
  { intros v s' R NS. applys seval_sound. applys M2.
    applys HC1. auto. auto. auto. }
Qed.
seval_seq.explanation: Sequencing for small-step seval: compose the evaluations of t1 and t2, threading the intermediate state.

seval_let.solution:
Lemma seval_let : forall s x t1 t2 Q1 Q,
  seval s t1 Q1 ->
  (forall v1 s2, Q1 v1 s2 -> seval s2 (subst x v1 t2) Q) ->
  seval s (trm_let x t1 t2) Q.
Proof using.
  introv M1 M2. destruct M1 as (HR1&HS1&HC1). splits.
  { intros s' t' R. inverts R; try solve [inverts H].
    tests C: (trm_is_val t1').
    { destruct C as (v1&->). lets (HR2&HS2&HC2): M2 (HC1 (steps_refl _ _)
        (fun t' s' R => inverts R; [false; inverts H | ])).
      inverts H0. applys HS2. applys steps_refl. }
    { applys HS1. auto. } }
  { lets (v&s'&R): HR1. exists v. exists s'. applys steps_of_step.
    applys step_let. }
  { intros v s' R NS. applys seval_sound. applys M2.
    applys HC1. auto. auto. auto. }
Qed.
seval_let.explanation: Let-binding for small-step seval: evaluate t1 to v1, substitute v1 for x in t2, then evaluate t2.

hoare_conseq.solution:
Lemma hoare_conseq : forall t H Q H' Q',
  hoare t H' Q' ->
  H ==> H' ->
  Q' ===> Q ->
  hoare t H Q.
Proof using.
  introv M MH MQ. intros h Hh.
  lets (v&h'&HR&HQ): M (MH _ Hh).
  exists v h'. splits. { auto. } { applys MQ HQ. }
Qed.
hoare_conseq.explanation: Consequence rule for Hoare triples: strengthen precondition, weaken postcondition.

btriple_frame.solution:
Lemma btriple_frame : forall t H Q H',
  btriple t H Q ->
  btriple t (H \* H') (Q \*+ H').
Proof using.
  introv M. unfolds btriple. intros H''. specializes M (H' \* H'').
  applys hoare_conseq M.
  { xsimpl. }
  { intros v. xsimpl. }
Qed.
btriple_frame.explanation: Frame rule for bundled triples: instantiate the universal quantifier with H' * H'' and adjust via consequence.

hoare_let.solution:
Lemma hoare_let : forall x t1 t2 H Q Q1,
  hoare t1 H Q1 ->
  (forall v, hoare (subst x v t2) (Q1 v) Q) ->
  hoare (trm_let x t1 t2) H Q.
Proof using.
  introv M1 M2. intros h Hh.
  lets (v1&h1&HR1&HQ1): M1 Hh.
  lets (v2&h2&HR2&HQ2): M2 HQ1.
  exists v2 h2. splits.
  { applys eval_let HR1 HR2. }
  { auto. }
Qed.
hoare_let.explanation: Let-binding for Hoare triples: evaluate t1 to get v1, substitute into t2, evaluate t2.

btriple_let.solution:
Lemma btriple_let : forall x t1 t2 Q1 H Q,
  btriple t1 H Q1 ->
  (forall v, btriple (subst x v t2) (Q1 v) Q) ->
  btriple (trm_let x t1 t2) H Q.
Proof using.
  introv M1 M2. unfolds btriple. intros H'.
  applys hoare_let.
  { applys M1. }
  { intros v. applys hoare_conseq. { applys M2. } { xsimpl. } { xsimpl. } }
Qed.
btriple_let.explanation: Let-binding for bundled triples: combine the Hoare let rule with the universal frame.
